nation: Chippewas of Kettle and Stony Point First Nation
auditor: Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants
one_time_description: Settlement proceeds from the Clench defalcation specific claim
economic_notes: Southwind Corporate Development Inc. operates corporate management and development with subsidiaries including Points Preference Supermarket Inc., Kettle and Stony Point Gas & Convenience Inc., and 9236058 Canada Inc. Indian Hills of Ipperwash Limited operates a golf course. COVID-19 pandemic noted as impacting operations.
notes: Auditor firm name not clearly stated - listed only as Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants based in London Ontario. Prior year comparative figures were restated due to related party transaction corrections. Large one-time settlement of $17,214,909 from Clench defalcation specific claim significantly inflated revenue and surplus. Restricted cash of $15,092,290 held for Clench settlement. Two GBEs (Southwind Corporate Development Inc. and Indian Hills of Ipperwash Limited) accounted for on modified equity basis. Two consolidated subsidiaries: Points Eagle Radio Inc. and Chippewas of Kettle and Stony Point Settlement Trust (2016). Portfolio investments of $113.9M largely restricted (Stony Point FSA Trust Fund $90.5M). OSR calculated as total revenue minus all government transfers including OFLP, SFNS, Stepping Stones, UOI, and other transfers recognized.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
